title: Install Zentauri UI for React & Next.js
description: Install @zentauri-ui/zentauri-components, wire Tailwind CSS v4, and import UI primitives into React or Next.js projects with predictable build settings.

og.title: Install Zentauri UI components
og.description: Step-by-step installation for Zentauri UI: package install, Tailwind v4, and Next.js-friendly imports.

twitter.title: Install Zentauri UI components
twitter.description: Step-by-step installation for Zentauri UI: package install, Tailwind v4, and Next.js-friendly imports.

intro: Wire the published package into a React or Next.js workspace, enable Tailwind CSS v4 tokens, and start importing UI primitives with stable paths.
useCases: Bootstrap a new Next.js design system workspace with shared Tailwind presets.
Add Zentauri UI to an existing React SPA bundler (Vite, webpack, or Rspack).
Align monorepo packages so apps consume a single version of the UI kit.
Document internal onboarding with reproducible install commands.
Prepare CI caches for faster installs of workspace-linked packages.

faqs.question: Which package name should I install?
faqs.answer: Use @zentauri-ui/zentauri-components from npm or link the workspace package during local development.

faqs.question: Is Tailwind CSS v4 required?
faqs.answer: The library targets Tailwind v4 class patterns; follow the snippets in this guide to align postcss and content globs.

faqs.question: Does this work with React Server Components?
faqs.answer: Yes for static markup; interactive demos opt into client components—mirror that split in your app.

sections.heading: What this guide covers
sections.body:
You will link the workspace package or install from npm, configure Tailwind v4 includes, and verify imports in a minimal React tree.

The goal is a repeatable setup developers can paste into internal docs.

sections.heading: Package layout and imports
sections.body:
Exports are organized under predictable entry points so tree-shaking stays effective.

Prefer path imports that match your bundler’s module resolution and avoid deep imports that bypass public API guarantees.

sections.heading: Accessibility and styling notes
sections.body:
Tailwind tokens should inherit from your app’s theme extension so contrast stays on-brand.

Keep global resets minimal to avoid fighting component-level focus styles.

sections.heading: Next.js integration notes
sections.body:
Use environment variables for site metadata and keep server components free of client-only animation drivers unless isolated.

Document any SSR constraints for portals or layered overlays.
